entries.title: Jacob Will Work For You
entries.summary: I grew as a generalist from a small office to a 10-figure acquisition. Hire me and we'll do it again.

entries.title: I’m Writing The Dating Sequences over at secondperson.dating
entries.summary: Going full-time on Putanumonit's favorite topic.

entries.title: Seth Explains Consciousness
entries.summary: Philosophers have long explained why science could never explain consciousness. Thankfully, science didn't listen.

entries.title: Whose dream bf is it anyway
entries.summary: Everyone wants to date someone impressive, but focusing on impressiveness in dating is perfectly useless.

entries.title: What’s Not Our Problem
entries.summary: A review of what is in Tim Urban's book on politics, and what very conspicuously isn't.

entries.title: Building and Entertaining Couples
entries.summary: People look for partners to build families with, but pick partners based on how entertaining they are after a few drinks. Both have value, but these are very different relationships.

entries.title: UDASSA
entries.summary: You are a random sample of short experience-generating strings, and the crazy thing about it is that it's all less crazy than it sounds.

entries.title: Why Are Women Hot?
entries.summary: Women are hot to seduce men. This is both plainly true and paradoxically contradicted everywhere men look at women. So what is actually going on?

entries.title: Date-me, Doc!
entries.summary: Documenting the rise of date-me docs and the decline of resultant dating

entries.title: Metacognition in the Rat
entries.summary: Awareness of your own uncertainty requires consciousness, and some claim that this awareness is present in rats. Are rats smarter than we thought, or is it the humans that are dumber?

entries.title: Genders Discrimination
entries.summary: Whales are queering the fish-mammal binary

entries.title: Tarnished Guy Who Puts A Num On It
entries.summary: Why Elden Ring is great, and how not to die binomially.

entries.title: Midwit Monogamy
entries.summary: A relationship must sacrifice exclusivity or sacrifice for the sake of exclusivity. Simply feeling entitled to it is a recipe for failure.

entries.title: Between the Blog and the Birdsite
entries.summary: Looking for a middle ground between the hard upsides of blogging and the easy downsides of tweeting.

entries.title: Artificial Wordcels
entries.summary: Humans think in concepts and perceive ourselves living in physical reality, but babies aren't born doing either. We learn these things slowly, and not by reading. So who will teach them to GPT?

entries.title: Selfless Dating
entries.summary: What if you just modeled your partner well enough to do what they would want without needing to ask? And what if you tried this from the first date?

entries.title: 10 Reasons You’re Lazy About Dating
entries.summary: Everyone complains that dating isn't working, but no one want to put the work into dating. Here are 10 reasons why.

entries.title: Consensual Hostility
entries.summary: In a world of conflict between men and women, consent negotiation is required. But the focus on consent to the exclusion of everything else is what brought this world about.

entries.title: Sex Versus
entries.summary: The NYT calls an end to sex-positivity blaming the patriarchy, and the trads agree while blaming the women. But I wouldn't give up on the opposite sex just yet.

entries.title: Rules for Noobs
entries.summary: Beginners need explicit rules, masters need to forget them. I propose a general model of learning, ask you not to "be yourself", speculate on AI, and teach you how to crush it on Twitter.
